title: campanopsis caledonica
intro: Campanopsis caledonica, also known as the New Caledonian harebell or Caledonia harebell, is a flowering plant species in the bellflower family that is native to New Caledonia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. It has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and can also be used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
